title: mirabilis elegans
intro: Mirabilis elegans, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican poppy, is a plant species in the family Nyctaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and combat fever. The plant's roots and leaves contain compounds with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including relief from headaches, joint pain, and skin irritations. Mirabilis elegans can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or ointment, allowing for various forms of use in traditional medicine.
